title: Carbon Audit Matrix Format (CAMF): a self-verifiable specification for product carbon footprint declarations
description:
CAMF is a format for publishing a product carbon footprint together with the computation that produced it: the production network as a table of edges, the extension vectors and the assumption set, canonically serialised and committed to by a SHA-256 Merkle root, so that any third party can recompute the declared figures exactly.

The deposit contains the normative specification (spec/CAMF-1.0.md, spec/canonicalization.md, JSON Schemas), a reference implementation under 500 lines, seven diagnostic checks, a worked example solvable by hand, four defect variants each caught by exactly one check, and an auditor checklist.

Licensing by content type: code (camf/, reproduce.py, tests/) under Apache-2.0, for its express patent grant and retaliation clause; specification text (spec/, README.md, audit/) under CC BY 4.0, so it can be quoted, translated and included in regulatory material; examples and data (examples/) under CC0 1.0. See LICENSES.md.

Published as a defensive publication.
